Your call.

📊 IBKR Intelligence

Account performance (TWR/MWR), portfolio allocation, investment themes, and peer data are all unavailable — IBKR connector offline this run. This section will populate on the next successful IBKR connection.

📚 Day 9 — Operating Leverage

When revenue grows faster than costs, operating profit grows even faster — that’s operating leverage. Fixed-cost businesses benefit disproportionately: ANET’s R&D, headcount, and infrastructure don’t scale linearly with revenue. So when AI demand pushes ANET’s quarterly revenue up 35% YoY, operating income can grow 50–60%. That gap is the multiplier. This is why ANET’s stock responds harder to hyperscaler capex signals than to any individual revenue print — the market prices the EPS leverage, not just the top line. ANET’s Q2 revenue guidance of $2.8B (+~28% YoY) while consensus EPS grows ~35–40% — that delta is operating leverage in action. The stop at $135 existed for the same reason in reverse: a hyperscaler capex cut wouldn’t just hit revenue 10%, it would compress earnings 25–30% through the same mechanism working against you.

Also today: Incremental margin — the operating margin on each additional dollar of revenue. ANET’s AI networking incremental margin is estimated at 50%+, well above its blended reported operating margin. High incrementals = operating leverage visible in real time.

Research only — not financial advice. All decisions are Abbas Al Madani’s own.
